user_claim: Customer: Hi, I found new damage on my car after it was parked outside overnight. | Support: Sorry to hear that. Can you describe what changed? | Customer: The back of the car has a dent now. It was not there before. | Support: Did anything else break or is it mostly body damage? | Customer: Mostly the rear bumper area. I attached the photo I took this morning.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image provides a clear, direct view of the car's rear, allowing for a full assessment of the damage to the bumper and trunk as required by the evidence standard.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows significant damage to the rear of the car, including a missing bumper cover and a large dent in the trunk lid, which is consistent with the user's claim of a dent in the rear bumper area.

user_claim: Customer: Parking lot mein meri car ko scrape lag gaya. | Support: Aap kis type ka damage report karna chahte hain? | Customer: Front side par mark aa gaya hai, bumper ke upar. | Support: Light damage hai ya body par scratch? | Customer: Light theek hai, front bumper par scratch hai. Photos upload kar diye hain.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The front bumper is visible in the submitted images, allowing for an assessment of its condition, although the images show two different vehicles.
claim_status_justification: The user claimed a 'scratch' on the front bumper, but the provided evidence is contradictory. img_1 shows a car with severe damage including dents and broken parts, which is more than a scratch. img_2 shows a completely different car model with no damage at all. Neither image supports the specific claim.

user_claim: Customer: I am opening a claim for my windshield. | Support: What happened? | Customer: A small stone hit it while I was driving and now there is a crack spreading from that spot. | Support: Is the car otherwise okay? | Customer: Yes, this is only about the front glass. I added the pictures I have.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The windshield is clearly visible in the provided images, and img_1 provides sufficient detail to assess the claimed damage, meeting the evidence standard.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows a shattered windshield, which supports the user's claim of a crack. However, img_2, submitted in the same claim, shows the windshield as completely undamaged. This direct contradiction is a significant risk factor and requires manual review.

user_claim: Customer: Someone clipped my car while it was parked. | Support: What part was hit? | Customer: The side mirror got damaged. It is not sitting the way it should anymore. | Support: Was there any damage to the door too? | Customer: I did not notice anything else major, just the mirror.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image provides a clear, close-up view of the car's side mirror, which is sufficient to verify the claimed damage.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows the glass of the side mirror is shattered, which directly supports the user's claim of a damaged mirror.

user_claim: Customer: I want to file this as bumper damage. | Support: Can you tell me what happened? | Customer: The car was tapped from behind and now the back looks damaged. | Support: Are you asking for review of the rear side? | Customer: Yes, the back bumper. It looks pretty bad to me, so I uploaded both photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The rear bumper is visible in the provided images, allowing for an assessment of its condition, although the images appear to be of two different vehicles.
claim_status_justification: The user's claim of 'pretty bad' damage is contradicted by the evidence. img_1 shows only minor, low-severity scratches. img_2 shows no damage at all and appears to be a different vehicle with an aftermarket bumper guard. The visual evidence does not support the claimed severity.

user_claim: Customer: Hi, I am not fully sure how to explain this because I noticed it only after reaching home. | Support: No problem, please tell me what happened. | Customer: There was a small bump earlier, nothing major, and I first thought everything was fine. Later I looked at the car again because my family member said the front area looked different. | Support: Are you reporting a general vehicle issue or a specific damaged part? | Customer: I was confused at first because I checked the side and the front and walked around the car a few times. I do not want to claim the wrong thing. | Support: That is okay. What part do you want reviewed? | Customer: After checking again, I think the issue is with the headlight. It looks like the headlight may be cracked, so please review that part.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image does not show the headlight, which is the part being claimed. Therefore, it is not possible to verify if it is cracked.
claim_status_justification: The user claimed a cracked headlight, but the provided image (img_1) only shows the side of the car. The headlight is not in the frame, so the claim cannot be assessed.

user_claim: Customer: There is a dent along the side of my car. | Support: When did you notice it? | Customer: This morning, after the car had been parked near the gate. | Support: Is it near the front or back? | Customer: It is on the door panel area. I attached two photos from different distances.
evidence_standard_met_reason: Although one image is blurry, the second image clearly shows the claimed damage on the car door, which is sufficient to meet the evidence standard for assessing body panel damage.
claim_status_justification: img_2 provides clear visual evidence of a significant dent and associated scratches on the car's rear door panel, which directly supports the user's claim. img_1 is too blurry to provide any information.

user_claim: Customer: I picked up my car after service and noticed a mark on the hood. | Support: What kind of mark is it? | Customer: It looks like a scratch across the top panel. | Support: Do you think it happened during service? | Customer: Yes, that is why I am asking for review. The photo is attached.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image clearly shows the car's hood and front end, allowing for an assessment of the damage, which contradicts the user's claim.
claim_status_justification: The user claimed a scratch on the hood, but img_1 shows the car has sustained catastrophic front-end damage, including a bent hood, missing bumper parts, and a crushed frame. This level of damage fundamentally contradicts the claim of a minor scratch from service.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop fell from the table yesterday. | Support: Is it turning on? | Customer: It turns on, but the display glass has a crack now. | Support: Are you reporting the screen only or the whole laptop? | Customer: The screen is the issue. I attached a photo of it open.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the claimed laptop part (screen) and the damage (severe cracking), meeting the evidence requirements.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows a shattered laptop screen, which directly supports the user's claim of a cracked display.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop no longer opens smoothly. | Support: What seems to be wrong mechanically? | Customer: The hinge area has broken and the screen wobbles. | Support: Did this happen after a drop? | Customer: Yes, it slipped from the sofa. I uploaded photos from the outside and side.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The evidence standard is met because img_1 provides a clear view of the claimed laptop part (hinge) and the damage is visible for assessment.
claim_status_justification: The claim for a broken hinge is supported by img_1, which shows a close-up of a laptop with a clearly broken hinge and detached screen bezel. However, img_2 appears to be a stock photo of a different, undamaged laptop, which raises risk and requires manual review.

user_claim: Customer: I spilled water near my laptop while working. | Support: What part was affected? | Customer: It went over the keys and left a stain. Some keys feel sticky now. | Support: Is the display damaged too? | Customer: No, this is about the keyboard area only.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The image provides a clear view of the laptop keyboard with a significant amount of liquid on it, directly corroborating the user's claim of a water spill.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a large quantity of liquid spilled across the laptop keyboard, which is consistent with the user's claim of water damage.

user_claim: Customer: My laptop bag fell from a chair and hit the floor. | Support: What damage did you notice? | Customer: One corner of the laptop has a dent now. | Support: Does the laptop still work? | Customer: It works, but the outer corner is damaged. I added two photos.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted images provide sufficient evidence, with img_2 clearly showing the claimed damage on the specified part of the object.
claim_status_justification: The user's claim of a dent on the laptop corner is directly supported by the visual evidence in img_2, which shows a clear, small dent on the corner of the laptop casing.

user_claim: Customer: I am not sure if this should be a repair claim or a replacement claim, so I wanted to ask first. | Support: We can help. What device is this about? | Customer: It is my laptop. I carry it every day, and yesterday my bag got pressed between two seats during travel. At first I thought the laptop was okay because it still opened. | Support: What changed after that? | Customer: Later, while using it, I noticed marks on the display area. I restarted it twice because I thought it might just be something on the screen, but it still bothered me. | Support: Are you reporting keyboard, hinge, body, or screen damage? | Customer: Not the keyboard or hinge. The issue I want checked is the screen. It looks shattered to me, so I am submitting this as screen damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the laptop screen, and the shattered glass is visible and consistent with the claim.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows a shattered screen with a spiderweb crack pattern, which directly supports the user's claim of screen damage.

user_claim: Customer: The laptop trackpad has stopped working properly. | Support: Did anything happen before it stopped working? | Customer: The front area hit the desk edge when I moved it. | Support: Are you reporting internal function or physical damage? | Customer: Physical damage around the trackpad area. I attached the photo for review.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the area around the laptop trackpad, and the physical damage is visible for assessment.
claim_status_justification: img_1 shows a small scratch on the laptop body adjacent to the trackpad, which is consistent with the user's claim of 'physical damage around the trackpad area'.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived damaged. | Support: What part of the package was affected? | Customer: One corner was crushed in when I received it. | Support: Is the item inside part of this claim? | Customer: Not right now. I am reporting the package corner damage.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image clearly shows the claimed package corner, and the crushing damage is visible and verifiable.
claim_status_justification: img_1 provides clear visual evidence of a crushed corner on the cardboard box, which directly supports the user's claim.

user_claim: Customer: Package receive hua toh opened jaisa lag raha tha. | Support: Tape broken tha ya box crush hua tha? | Customer: Seal wali side phati hui thi, jaise parcel khola gaya ho. | Support: Kya andar ka item missing hai? | Customer: Abhi item missing claim nahi kar raha, sirf torn packaging review karwana hai.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The submitted image set contains a clear photo (img_1) showing the claimed damage to the package seal, which is sufficient to meet the evidence standard.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows a cardboard package with its tape seal and surrounding cardboard torn open, which directly supports the user's claim. img_2 appears to be a generic stock photo of an undamaged box and is disregarded as non-original evidence, but its presence warrants manual review.

user_claim: Customer: I received a package that looks water damaged. | Support: Was the item inside wet too? | Customer: I have not opened everything yet, but the outside has a wet-looking stain. | Support: So the claim is about the package condition? | Customer: Yes, the package surface looked damaged when it arrived.
evidence_standard_met_reason: No usable images were provided — all images failed quality checks.
claim_status_justification: img_1 clearly shows a large, dark stain on the side and corner of the cardboard box, which is consistent with the user's claim of water damage.

user_claim: Customer: The item I ordered was not inside the box. | Support: Did the package look opened when you received it? | Customer: I checked it after delivery and could not find the product inside. | Support: What are you asking us to verify? | Customer: Please verify that the contents are missing from the package.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The images do not meet the evidence standard for a missing item claim, as img_1 shows the box filled with packing material obstructing the view, and img_2 shows the sealed exterior.
claim_status_justification: The claim of missing contents cannot be verified. img_1 shows the box filled with packing material, which obstructs the view of the bottom of the box. img_2 shows the exterior of the sealed package and is not relevant to the claim.

user_claim: Customer: The shipping box arrived in bad condition. | Support: What kind of condition issue are you reporting? | Customer: It looked badly crushed when it was delivered. | Support: Is the claim about the outside box or the product inside? | Customer: The outside box. I want the crushed box reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The user claimed the shipping box was crushed, but the image provided shows a dented can (the contents), not the box itself. The condition of the claimed object cannot be verified.
claim_status_justification: The claim is for a crushed 'outside box', but the image provided (img_1) shows a dented can, which appears to be the package contents. As the claimed object is not depicted, the claim cannot be verified.

user_claim: Customer: My delivery box arrived opened. | Support: Was the package crushed or was the seal affected? | Customer: The seal area looked torn when I received it. | Support: Are you asking us to review the package condition or the item inside? | Customer: The package condition. I want the torn-open package reviewed.
evidence_standard_met_reason: The evidence submission is invalid. It contains conflicting images, including one that appears to be a stock photo (img_2) and another with adversarial text (img_1), failing the evidence trust requirement.
claim_status_justification: The evidence is invalid for automated review. img_1 shows a torn seal but contains adversarial text. img_2 is a contradictory stock photo of an undamaged box. Due to the untrustworthy and conflicting nature of the evidence, the claim cannot be verified.
